---
title: "Exercise 6 — Fork, Branch & Pull Request"
format: html
---

**Module 4 | ~30 minutes**

---

## Goal

Contribute to the course repository using the real open-source collaboration workflow.

---

## The Workflow

```
DQBM-SIB/intro_git_github  (original)
        ↓  Fork on GitHub
YOUR_USERNAME/intro_git_github  (your copy)
        ↓  Clone
Your local machine
        ↓  Branch → Edit → Commit → Push
YOUR_USERNAME/intro_git_github  (your copy)
        ↓  Pull Request
DQBM-SIB/intro_git_github  (original)
```

---

## Part A — Fork

1. Go to `https://github.com/DQBM-SIB/intro_git_github`
2. Click **Fork** (top right) → select your account
3. You now have `https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/intro_git_github`

---

## Part B — Clone your fork

```bash
cd ~/Downloads

git clone git@github.com:YOUR_USERNAME/intro_git_github.git
cd intro_git_github
git log --oneline    # see the existing history
```

---

## Part C — Branch

```bash
git checkout -b YOUR_FIRST_NAME
git branch     # confirm
```

---

## Part D — Add your project description

```bash
nano projects.md
```

Add a new section:

```markdown
# Your Full Name
Brief description of your research project.
What question are you answering? What data or tools do you use?
```

```bash
git add projects.md
git status
git commit -m "added YOUR_NAME project description"
```

---

## Part E — Push and open a Pull Request

```bash
git push origin YOUR_FIRST_NAME
```

On GitHub:

1. Go to `https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/intro_git_github`
2. Click **Compare & pull request**
3. Base: `DQBM-SIB/intro_git_github` → `main`
4. Title: `Added YOUR_NAME project description`
5. Description: `Closes #YOUR_ISSUE_NUMBER`
6. Click **Create pull request**

---

## Part F — After all PRs are merged

```bash
# Add the original repo as upstream
git remote add upstream git@github.com:DQBM-SIB/intro_git_github.git

# Get everyone's merged contributions
git fetch upstream
git checkout main
git merge upstream/main

git log --oneline    # see all contributions in one place
```

---

## What you learned

| Term | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| Fork | Server-side copy of a repo under your username |
| Clone | Local copy of a repo |
| Upstream | The original repo you forked from |
| Pull Request | Proposal to merge your branch |
